channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Saskatchewan grain producers urge federal government to improve export sales transparency to boost market fairness
item.description: Grain farmers in Saskatchewan are calling on the federal government to require greater transparency from grain exporters, arguing that a lack of timely sales data is costing them millions of dollars i...

item.title: Fisantekraal volunteers plant indigenous trees to restore Mosselbank River wetlands
item.description: Volunteers from the Fisantekraal community, a township located roughly ten kilometres northeast of Durbanville in the Western Cape, gathered despite rainy weather on Saturday to take part in a large-s...

item.title: IGP Disu Restructures Police Monitoring Unit, Transfers Personnel Linked to Fakorede
item.description: Ahahn! Things don shift for the Nigeria Police Force, abeg! The Inspector-General of Police, Tunji Disu, don order the dismantling of the Special Investigation Unit (SIU) and ordered the transfer of a...

item.title: Maharashtra temporarily halts iron ore mining project in Chandrapur due to ecological concerns near Tadoba tiger corridor
item.description: The Maharashtra government has temporarily halted the Lohardongri iron ore mining project located in the Bramhapuri forest division of Chandrapur district, following sustained protests by environmenta...

item.title: Outa urges City Power to simplify approval process for residential solar installations
item.description: The Organisation Undoing Tax Abuse (Outa) has criticised Johannesburg’s City Power over its newly introduced pre-approval and registration requirements for small-scale embedded generation (SSEG) syste...

item.title: Fuel prices in the Philippines see significant rollback on April 21 amid easing global oil pressures
item.description: The latest fuel price update in the Philippines shows a significant rollback in pump prices effective Tuesday, April 21, 2026, as global oil market pressures continue to ease, particularly due to redu...

item.title: Firefighting efforts continue at Navotas sanitary landfill as smoke affects Metro Manila air quality
item.description: Firefighting operations at the Navotas Sanitary Landfill are ongoing after a blaze broke out on April 10, 2026, with authorities working continuously to suppress the smoldering fire. The incident has ...

item.title: The 55th Anniversary of Salyut 1 and the Origins of the First Space Station
item.description: The article reviews the 55-year legacy of Salyut 1, launched on April 19, 1971, as the world’s first space station. Lifted into orbit by a Proton-K rocket, Salyut 1—initially designated DOS-1 within t...

item.title: Patrick Mahomes begins off-season rehab and joins Chiefs’ programme after knee ligament injury
item.description: Kansas City Chiefs quarterback Patrick Mahomes has taken part in the opening phase of the team’s off-season programme while continuing his recovery from significant knee injuries. Mahomes suffered tea...

item.title: Mwende Macharia returns to radio with Radio Jambo as station ranks among Nairobi’s top Swahili broadcasters
item.description: Veteran radio presenter Mwende Macharia has made a comeback to the airwaves after about 15 months away, joining Radio Jambo as a late-night show host. She expressed excitement about the new role, sayi...

item.title: Five men arrested after alleged abduction and vehicle fire in Western Sydney
item.description: Five men have been arrested following an alleged violent abduction of a man in Western Sydney, with police investigating a possible link to a burnt-out vehicle discovered shortly after the incident. A...

item.title: PM Modi joins South Korean President and Samsung Chairman for Galaxy Z Flip7 selfie during India–Korea business meet
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi was seen sharing a light moment with South Korean President Lee Jae Myung and Samsung Electronics Executive Chairman Lee Jae-yong during a state luncheon held in New Delhi...

item.title: Controversy over Haaland goal in Manchester City win over Arsenal amid alleged foul on Gabriel
item.description: A fresh video analysis has sparked debate over Erling Haaland’s winning goal for Manchester City against Arsenal in a Premier League clash at the Etihad Stadium. The match, which had major implication...

item.title: Industrial applications of dry ice blasting across food processing and manufacturing sectors
item.description: The article explores how dry ice blasting has become an increasingly important cleaning method across various industries, particularly in food processing plants and heavy manufacturing environments. U...

item.title: Woman Dies After Collapsing at UTME Centre in Ondo State

item.title: India withdraws plan to preinstall Aadhaar ID app on smartphones after industry concerns
item.description: The Indian government has decided to drop a proposal that would have required smartphone manufacturers like Apple and Samsung to preinstall the country’s Aadhaar national ID application on new devices...

item.title: Curated bridal colour combinations that give a luxury and modern wedding aesthetic
item.description: This article explores a carefully selected list of bridal colour combinations that give off a rich, elegant and modern wedding vibe without looking overdone or too common. It focuses on how brides can...

item.title: PSX declines 1,742 points as geopolitical tensions pressure market
item.description: The Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX) witnessed a sharp downturn on Monday as the benchmark KSE-100 Index fell by 1,742.31 points, or around 1%, closing at 172,196.70 compared to the previous close of 173...

item.title: US Labour Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer resigns amid misconduct allegations and internal investigation
item.description: Lori Chavez-DeRemer has stepped down from her role as US Labour Secretary, with the White House confirming her departure from President Donald Trump’s cabinet. Her exit comes amid a series of allegati...

item.title: Pakistan and Egypt Joint Military Exercise 'Thunder-II' Concludes
item.description: Pakistan and Egypt recently wrapped up a two-week-long joint military exercise called “Pakistan-Egypt Joint Exercise Thunder-II,” according to a statement released by the Inter-Services Public Relatio...
